question: According to Table 5.2-2, what is the RSV capacity in lbm/hr?

nuscale_ch05_0009.title: Overpressure Protection
nuscale_ch05_0009.summary: This section outlines NuScale's compliance with NRC regulations and ASME code requirements for overpressure protection of the reactor coolant boundary. It details the use of specific ASME BPVC and OM code cases for design, fabrication, inspection, and testing, ensuring all selected code cases meet NRC-endorsed conditions as specified in relevant regulatory guides and federal regulations. Table 5.2-1 consolidates the specific code cases applied.

nuscale_ch05_0007.title: Integrity of Reactor Coolant Boundary
nuscale_ch05_0007.summary: This section describes the design features of each NuScale Power Module (NPM) that ensure the integrity of the reactor coolant pressure boundary (RCPB) throughout the plant’s design life, in accordance with regulatory definitions. It details the components and systems included in the RCPB, such as the reactor pressure vessel, containment isolation valves, safety valves, and associated piping, emphasizing their roles in maintaining pressure containment and system safety.

nuscale_ch05_0008.title: Compliance with Codes and Code Cases
nuscale_ch05_0008.summary: This section describes how the NuScale Power Module (NPM) and its reactor coolant pressure boundary (RCPB) components comply with relevant U.S. regulations, specifically 10 CFR 50.55a and associated General Design Criteria. It details that these components are designed, fabricated, and tested to ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code (BPVC) Section III standards, with specific classifications for different piping systems, and references the applicable ASME BPVC 2013 Edition as the code of record.

nuscale_ch05_0013.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0013.summary: This section lists references and standards related to the integrity of the reactor coolant boundary in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report. It includes guidelines and reports from the Electric Power Research Institute and the American Society of Mechanical Engineers, as well as specific ASME code cases relevant to reactor component materials, quality assurance, and inspection procedures.

nuscale_ch05_0025.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0025.summary: This section details the materials used in the NuScale reactor pressurizer, specifying the types of steel and alloys for each component, such as the baffle plate, heater bundles, cover plate, and bolting, all chosen for corrosion resistance and compatibility with operating environments. It also references relevant material specifications from the ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code (BPVC) to ensure compliance and safety.

nuscale_ch05_0000.title: Preface
nuscale_ch05_0000.summary: The Preface outlines the copyright and usage restrictions for the NuScale Standard Plant Design Certification Application, specifically Chapter Five on the Reactor Coolant System and Connecting Systems. It specifies that only the U.S. Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC) is authorized to use, copy, or disclose the document for regulatory purposes, and any copies must retain the copyright notice. The section also references the table of contents for Chapter Five, which covers the summary description and design basis of the system.

nuscale_ch05_0019.title: Reactor Coolant System Component and Subsystem Design
nuscale_ch05_0019.summary: This section describes the design of the NuScale Power Module’s Reactor Coolant System (RCS) components and subsystems, including the reactor pressure vessel, steam generators, safety and emergency valves, and associated piping. It details how the steam generators are integrated into the reactor vessel, forming part of the primary and secondary pressure boundaries, and references other sections for further information on specific components.

edges.description: Section A describes regulatory codes and testing programs relevant to overpressure protection, while Section B outlines the design features that maintain the integrity of the reactor coolant pressure boundary, both of which are connected through the requirement to comply with regulatory standards for reactor safety.
Section A provides details about the specific ASME BPVC code cases and compliance provisions referenced in Section B's general statement of compliance with 10 CFR 50.55a and ASME BPVC requirements.

Section A discusses regulatory compliance and code cases relevant to overpressure protection, while Section B lists references that may provide supporting standards or guidelines, indicating that Section A's compliance statements are supported by the documents cited in Section B.

Section A describes the design features that maintain the integrity of the reactor coolant pressure boundary, while Section B specifies that these features comply with regulatory codes and standards, connecting the design implementation (A) with regulatory compliance (B).

Section A discusses the design features and scope of the reactor coolant pressure boundary, while Section B lists references that provide supporting technical standards and guidelines relevant to those design features.

Section A discusses compliance with regulatory codes for reactor coolant boundary integrity, while Section B lists references including ASME and EPRI documents that support or provide the basis for the compliance claims made in Section A.

Section A describes the physical components and materials of the pressurizer, while Section B outlines the regulatory codes and standards (such as ASME and 10 CFR 50.55a) that these components must comply with, connecting them through the requirement that the pressurizer's design and materials meet specific regulatory provisions.
Section A provides detailed descriptions of pressurizer components and their materials, which are part of the reactor coolant pressure boundary whose integrity is discussed in Section B.

Section A describes the materials and components of the pressurizer, while Section B states that these components comply with ASME BPVC and regulatory requirements, connecting the design details in A to the compliance claims in B.

Section A is the preface providing publication and copyright information for the NuScale Standard Plant Design Certification Application, while Section B begins the technical discussion on the integrity of the reactor coolant boundary; thus, Section A provides structural context and document provenance for the technical content in Section B.

Section A identifies the reactor safety valves (RSVs) as components within the reactor coolant system, while Section B discusses the regulatory codes and standards (ASME BPVC, 10 CFR 50.55a) that govern the design and testing of such components, thereby connecting the physical component (RSV) to its regulatory requirements.

Section A lists the reactor safety valves (RSVs) as components within the reactor coolant system, while Section B defines the reactor coolant pressure boundary (RCPB) to include pressure-retaining components such as the RSVs, thus connecting the identification of RSVs in A to their regulatory boundary context in B.

Section A describes the components of the reactor coolant system, including the reactor safety valves (RSVs) whose capacity is asked about in the analysis question, while Section B establishes that these components, including RSVs, must comply with regulatory codes such as ASME BPVC as required by 10 CFR 50.55a.

Section A provides the general document context and copyright information, while Section B contains the technical content relevant to the analysis question about RSV capacity, making Section A a prerequisite for understanding the scope and authority of Section B.
